answers: Initializes a new local Git repository

questions.text: Which command stages all changes for the next commit?
What is the correct command to create and switch to a new branch?

answers: Pushes changes to a stash remote

questions.text: Which of the following are valid git merge strategies?
What does a `detached HEAD` state mean?
answers: You are not on any branch but on a specific commit
The latest commit has no message
Your local branch is behind the remote

questions.text: What is the difference between `git merge` and `git rebase`?
answers: Merge preserves history, rebase rewrites it onto another base
Rebase only works on remote branches
Merge always creates a merge commit
Applies a specific commit from another branch
Moves all commits to a new branch

questions.text: What does `git bisect` help you do?
answers: Split a large commit into smaller ones
Find the commit that introduced a bug
